retrieved_docs.title: Safe and Effective Use of Baricitinib and Remdesivir in Hospitalized Patients With Coronavirus (COVID-19) -- Function -- Anti-inflammatory properties of Baricitinib
retrieved_docs.content: Baricitinib is a selective and reversible inhibitor of Janus Kinases (JAK) JAK1 and JAK2 originally approved for use in individuals with rheumatoid arthritis (RA). Its use has been justified by proven dampening of the inflammatory response [5] . The JAK/STAT pathway has been identified as one of the subcellular pathways that mediate signal transduction of external stimuli to the nuclei of the cell, thereby mediating normal as well as aberrant immune responses. When cytokines attach to their receptors on the surface of the cell, a conformational change in the receptor leads to activation of the associated JAK complex. Once activated, the JAK complex autophosphorylates, creating a docking site for signaling molecules of the STAT family. The activated JAK complex also shows increased Janus Kinase activity leading to further phosphorylation of the STAT signaling pathway. The phosphorylated STAT molecules are then released from the receptor, translocate to the nucleus and lead to DNA sequence activation. This ultimately leads to target gene transcription [6] . The JAK signaling pathway family includes 4 proteins, the JAK1, JAK2, JAK3, and tyrosine kinase 2 (TYK2) [7] . Disruption of one or more of the JAK proteins is associated with an interruption in the signal transduction pathway and can lead to the beneficial effects of dampening a heightened immune response [8] . Cytokines implicated in causing the cytokine storm like the IFN-γ, IL-6, etc. have a JAK-mediated mechanism of action. IL6 is associated with JAK1, JAK2, and TYK2 [9] , whereas IFN-γ activity is mediated by JAK1/JAK2 [10] . Stebbing and colleagues validated the above-mentioned mechanism in their study. On evaluating the in-vitro pharmacology in relevant leucocytes as well as in-vivo pharmacokinetics of Baricitinib, they were able to demonstrate inhibition of IL-2, IL-6, IL-10, IFN-γ, and GM-CSF [11] . See Figure. Baricitinib Mechanism of Action

retrieved_docs.content: Despite the diverse primary amino acid sequences, human kinases share similar 3D structures, particularly when it comes to the ATP-binding pocket located in the catalytically active region. The starting amino acid sequence (ASP-Phe-Gly or DFG) of the flexible activation loop that controls access to the activation site is also typically conserved. [41]

retrieved_docs.title: Biochemistry, Tertiary Protein Structure -- Fundamentals -- Tertiary Structure of Proteins
retrieved_docs.content: Covalent bonding can occur between the side chains of 2 cysteines through a reaction involving their sulfhydryl groups. These disulfide bonds are often called bridges because they create strong links between distinct regions of the same polypeptide or 2 separate polypeptides. Ionic bonding, on the other hand, forms between groups with full and opposing charges, such as the ionized acidic and basic R groups.

retrieved_docs.title: Biochemistry, Tertiary Protein Structure -- Fundamentals -- Secondary Structure of Proteins
retrieved_docs.content: In an α-helix, hydrogen bonds are formed between residues, which are only 4 linear positions apart. The distance between residues that form a β-pleated sheet can be more significant as folds in the chain bring them close enough to bond in a 3-dimensional space.

retrieved_docs.title: Biochemistry, Protein Targeting and I Cell Diseases -- Clinical Significance -- Bony and Radiological Abnormalities
retrieved_docs.content: Dysostosis multiplex is the broad term that encompasses the skeletal abnormalities of ICD. General x-ray findings are demineralization, osteopenia, coarse trabecular bones, and periosteal cloaking (predominantly long bones). [65] [76] X-rays of the upper limbs may show metacarpal pointing and bullet-shaped phalanges. Pelvic radiographs may show iliac flaring. A J-shaped sella turcica may also be visualized. [73] Bone dysplasia with shortened and curved bones is considered a prenatal manifestation of ICD. Deformities of the vertebral bodies may show anterior beaking, leading to abnormal spinal curvatures. [33] The cervical spine vertebral bodies are normal, whereas the dorsal and lumbar spine vertebral bodies have a reduced anteroposterior diameter. In ICD, the bones of the upper limb are more affected than those of the lower limb. The hypoplastic carpal bones and normal tarsal bones exemplify this. [76]

retrieved_docs.title: Biochemistry, Tertiary Protein Structure -- Fundamentals -- Tertiary Structure of Proteins
retrieved_docs.content: A protein's distinctive 3-dimensional configuration, or tertiary structure, arises from interactions between residues as the chain bends and folds in a 3-dimensional space. These interacting residues are often distant from each other in the linear sequence.

retrieved_docs.title: MALToma -- Pathophysiology
retrieved_docs.content: These translocations lead to the constitutive activation of the NF-κB pathway, which stimulates the release of cytokines and enhances cell survival. Alternatively, MALTomas with t(3:14) lead to the overexpression of FOX1P, a transcriptional suppressor of caspase-dependent pro-apoptotic genes. Other chromosomal aberrations, such as trisomies 3, 12, and 18, have also been reported. Although they may not activate NF-κB directly, they utilize alternative pathways to resist apoptosis and promote proliferation. Moreover, somatic mutations, such as missense and frameshift mutations in cMyc and PIM1 , have also been documented.

retrieved_docs.title: Physiology, Tyrosine Kinase Receptors -- Mechanism -- Activation of Cytoplasmic Kinase Domains
retrieved_docs.content: TKDs of RTKs are comprised of a C-lobe, N-lobe, and "activation loop." For a TKD to become functional, both the "activation loop" and N-lobe alpha-C helix must adopt a specific, "active" configuration. In the majority of cases, the release of cis-autoinhibition is required for this to occur. Cis-autoinhibition occurs in several ways. In Ins receptors, a tyrosine on the activation loop occupies the kinase active site, forming a stable configuration that blocks ATP and protein substrate entry.

retrieved_docs.title: Biochemistry, Primary Protein Structure -- Molecular Level
retrieved_docs.content: After a mature mRNA transcript leaves the nucleus, a ribosome attaches to the transcript to begin translation. The initiation site in both prokaryotes and eukaryotes is not composed of a unique initiation sequence but is more probably a multisubstrate enzyme system based on substrate specificity. [5] [6] [7] [8] A postulated mechanism (scanning mechanism) predicts that the ribosomes initiate protein synthesis by recognizing and binding the 5′ tail of the mRNA corresponding to a 7-methylguanosine referred to as "cap." [9] The first methionine codon located in the 5′ ends of the mRNA is the initiator codon. Several factors bind to this initiation sequence (IF4F initiation factors), forming the ribosome-eIF2-GTP-Met-tRNA preinitiation complex (PIC). [10] A favorable initiation sequence in mammals is known as “Kozak consensus”: 5’ (A/G)CCAUGG 3’. The PIC scans the mRNA in the 5’ region until it reaches the AUG start codon complementary to Met-tRNA's anticodon. The first AUG codon (“leaky scanning”) can be skipped to use a downstream AUG. This creates protein isoforms when the downstream AUG is located in the same open reading frame as the first 1 or a completely different protein if it is not in-frame.

retrieved_docs.title: Deucravacitinib -- Mechanism of Action
retrieved_docs.content: Deucravacitinib binds allosterically to the regulatory domain of Tyk2 (JH2, or pseudokinase), a region that is typically catalytically inactive. The drug induces an inhibitory conformation change involving the locking of Tyk2's JH2 (regulatory) domain with its JH1 (catalytic) domain. Thus, deucravacitinib traps the Tyk2 protein in an inactive state. [19] [20]

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 2528
retrieved_docs.content: Flibanserin is chemically linked to the benzimidazoly scaffold, indicating that its structure is derived from or mapped from benzimidazoly. In addition, flibanserin’s pharmacologic activity is mediated through inhibition of P‑glycoprotein, as it functions as a P‑glycoprotein inhibitor. This dual information—structural mapping to benzimidazoly and a mechanism of action involving P‑glycoprotein inhibition—provides insight into flibanserin’s pharmacokinetic behavior, potential drug‑drug interactions, and guides its clinical application.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 31 -- Level 3 -- Community 1907
retrieved_docs.content: Dechalinium and pyrvinium are both classified as quinoline compounds. As members of the quinoline chemical class, they share the characteristic heterocyclic structure that defines quinolines, which can influence their pharmacological properties and potential therapeutic applications. Recognizing that dechalinium and pyrvinium belong to the quinoline family aids in understanding their mechanisms of action, informs drug development, and supports informed decisions in patient care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 1231
retrieved_docs.content: Abacavir, an antiretroviral nucleoside reverse transcriptase inhibitor used in HIV treatment, is mapped to dideoxyadenosine, indicating a direct chemical or functional correspondence between the two nucleoside analogues. This mapping highlights the structural similarity of abacavir to dideoxyadenosine, which can be relevant for understanding its mechanism of action, potential cross‑reactivity, and the design of related therapeutic agents.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 4 -- Level 3 -- Community 2325
retrieved_docs.content: Benzimidazole is a heterocyclic scaffold that forms part of the chemical structures of both dabigatran etexilát, an oral anticoagulant, and lomitapide, a lipid‑lowering agent. This shared core illustrates how a single molecular framework can be incorporated into drugs with very different therapeutic targets, underscoring the versatility of benzimidazole in medicinal chemistry and its relevance to the design and understanding of diverse pharmacologic agents.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 7 -- Level 4 -- Community 2856
retrieved_docs.content: Benzimidazole is a heterocyclic aromatic scaffold that forms the core structure of a broad class of chemical compounds. Omeprazol is classified as a benzimidazole derivative, meaning it contains the benzimidazole core within its molecular structure. This relationship indicates that omeprazol belongs to the larger benzimidazole family, a fact that is important for understanding its pharmacologic properties and mechanism of action as a proton pump inhibitor.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 6 -- Level 3 -- Community 2378
retrieved_docs.content: Pyridine and/or pyridine derivative, guaietolin, and the generic category Ring Compound are all classified as heterocyklické sloučeniny (heterocyclic compounds). Each of these entities possesses a cyclic molecular structure that includes one or more heteroatoms such as nitrogen, oxygen, or sulfur within the ring. Recognizing that pyridine derivatives, guaietolin, and other ring compounds belong to the broader class of heterocyclic compounds is important for understanding their chemical behavior, potential pharmacological activity, and role in drug development, as heterocyclic scaffolds frequently contribute to the biological properties of therapeutic agents.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 28 -- Level 2 -- Community 269
retrieved_docs.content: The NCIt COVID‑19 Terminology ontology lists both omeprazol and ritonavir as included concepts, indicating that these drugs are recognized within the COVID‑19 knowledge framework. Omeprazol is classified as a benzimidazole derivative, as it is a subclass of the chemical family benzimidazole. Similarly, flibanserin is also a benzimidazole derivative, being a subclass of benzimidazole, and a related concept, benzimidazoly, is mapped from flibanserin, reflecting a close semantic connection between these entities. In addition to its chemical classification, flibanserin functions as a P‑glycoprotein inhibitor, suggesting it can influence drug transport and disposition. Together, these relationships highlight how omeprazol and ritonavir are positioned within a COVID‑19–focused terminology, while flibanserin’s benzimidazole structure and P‑glycoprotein inhibition provide insight into its pharmacological profile and potential interactions with other therapies.

Recognizing that cilostazol belongs to these chemical families helps elucidate its pharmacological properties, potential drug interactions, and the rationale for its therapeutic effects.
